description: Water falling from clouds in drops, nourishing the earth below. 'Pheralu' names the essential gift from sky to soil—the rhythmic blessing that fills rivers, feeds plants, and renews the cycle of life. Both the phenomenon and the act of raining.
sound_symbolism: The word falls like gentle drops. 'Phe' /ɸe/ begins softly, water forming in clouds. Then 'ra' taps rhythmically, the patter of drops. Finally 'lu' pools and flows, water gathering on earth.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'pheralu' (the rain). Rain falls: 'pheralu lepa.' - the language's own idiom. Central to agricultural and seasonal vocabulary; related to 'phialu' (water) through shared liquid meaning.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Rain is the renewable gift that makes terrestrial life possible—harvesting rainwater honors this cycle of sky-to-earth nourishment.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures celebrated rain through ceremony and gratitude, recognizing dependence on this blessing from above.
